creator: kreinholz@gmail.com
text:
I updated my amd64 system from FreeBSD 11.2-RELEASE to 12.0-RELEASE on 11 December 2018, the RELEASE announcement date. Now, rust-cbindgen will not build, and I am unable to update Firefox (I previously had version 64 installed on FreeBSD 11.2-RELEASE, but it was downgraded to version 63 during reinstallation of all installed packages during the freebsd-update process).

It appears that an old bug pertaining to FreeBSD 12.0-CURRENT affected rust due to a change in the memory layout of stat in FreeBSD 12:

https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=229826

https://github.com/jwilm/alacritty/issues/618

As stated by lukaslueg in a 15 June 2017 post: "The memory layout of stat has changed with FreeBSD 12, which causes rust's os-specific wrapper of stat to mangle its own stack and end up in 0x0000000000000000, giving you a blank backtrace in the process."

The last update to rust-cbindgen was on 6 December 2018, prior to the public announcement of FreeBSD 12.0-RELEASE, so perhaps the issue was not caught. I have the current version of rust (1.31.0) installed, but will try switching to rust-nightly to see if this helps rust-cbindgen build without segfaulting during the configure script running. I've rebuilt all installed packages on my system as recommended during the upgrade from FreeBSD 11.2 to 12.0, and subsequently updated 300+ packages from Ports (since the required reinstallation of all third party packages in the middle of freebsd-update left me with older versions of many applications). 

rust-cbindgen, sadly, is one of a tiny handful of Port build "fails" since upgrading to FreeBSD 12.0-RELEASE. Chromium is good and all, but I like Firefox and would like to be able to build it. :)
